name: Cornbread Quiche Squares
recipeIngredient: 10 1/2 ounces asparagus, canned and drained (cut into 1-inch pieces)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F.
2. Unroll dough into a rectangle and place it in an ungreased 13x9-inch pan. Press the dough over the bottom and up the sides of the pan, forming a crust.
3. Firmly press perforations with a fork to seal. Bake at 375°F for 5 minutes (the dough will look underdone).
4. Remove from oven and spoon asparagus, pimiento, and onions evenly over the crust.
5. In a medium bowl, combine half-and-half, mustard, ginger, crushed red pepper, and eggs; mix well. Pour this mixture over the crust.
6. Sprinkle cheeses evenly over the egg mixture.
7. Bake at 375°F for 20 to 30 minutes, or until a knife inserted in the center comes out clean.
8. Cut into squares. Serve warm. Makes 24 appetizers (100 calories, 6g fat). [SW Variation: Substitute roasted and diced zucchini for the asparagus. Serve with a chunky, fresh salsa.]
